The invisible arc of power: How ballistic missiles reshaped modern warfare
description: Ballistic missiles, born from wartime innovation, revolutionized warfare by making distance irrelevant. These rocket-powered weapons, capable of delivering payloads across continents in minutes, evolved into the backbone of modern strategic deterrence. Understanding their history, function, and global proliferation is crucial to grasping today's security environment.

Panay highlighted the passion and AI advancements seen during his visit, underscoring India's vital role in building future products.

Rasgulla stuck in throat: Man chokes to death at wedding feast
description: A tragic incident unfolded at a wedding feast in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, where a 41-year-old man, Lalit Singh, succumbed to death. A rasgulla reportedly became lodged in his throat, completely blocking his airway and cutting off oxygen supply. He collapsed during the meal and tragically passed away within minutes despite immediate efforts to seek medical assistance.

title: 'When they are shot, I will be at peace': Delhi Uttam Nagar murder victim's mother demands justice
description: A 26-year-old man was killed in Delhi's Uttam Nagar after a Holi celebration dispute over a water balloon escalated into violence. His mother demands strict action, alleging the accused threatened her granddaughter and assaulted her family. The family seeks severe punishment for the perpetrators.

title: Nvidia CEO seemingly 'realises' that Google, Microsoft and Meta are set to eat lunch
description: Nvidia's dominance built on a single chip for all AI tasks is facing a challenge.  As inference, the running of AI models, becomes more crucial and cost-sensitive, customers are exploring cheaper, purpose-built alternatives from rivals like Google and Meta.  Jensen Huang's expected unveiling of a new inference-focused chip signals a potential shift away from Nvidia's long-held strategy.

title: Breathless after climbing stairs? Cardiologist explains heart warning signs
description: Taking the stairs could be a straightforward way to assess your heart health. Experts emphasize that your breathing and heart rate's recovery time post-exercise reflects your cardiovascular condition. If you find it takes longer than expected to bounce back, it might be a sign of stiff arteries or heart issues, highlighting the need for medical evaluation to safeguard your health.

title: Medico suicide case: 5 AIIMS Rajkot students arrested; 17-page note alleges assault by batchmates
description: Five final-year MBBS students from AIIMS Rajkot were arrested for abetting the suicide of a classmate, Ratan Kumar Meghwal. Meghwal died by train on March 14, leaving a 17-page note detailing alleged physical assault and mental harassment by the accused since January.

title: Maria Sharapova and Luka Doncic's photo goes viral after Lakers’ OT win
description: Tennis icon Maria Sharapova made a surprise appearance at an NBA game and later posed for a photo with Luka Dončić after the Lakers’ thrilling overtime win. The photo quickly went viral online.

title: Radko Gudas hit ends Auston Matthews’ season, sparks Crosby frustration
description: The NHL handed Radko Gudas a five-game suspension after a kneeing play left Auston Matthews with a season-ending MCL tear. The incident also revived anger tied to Sidney Crosby’s earlier knee injury, which forced him to miss Canada’s Olympic gold medal game. Both the Pittsburgh Penguins and Toronto Maple Leafs now face major lineup gaps, and many fans believe the punishment does little to reflect the long-term damage caused by the collisions.

title: Fans mock Travis Kelce after he credits Taylor Swift for NFL comeback
description: Travis Kelce revealed that fiancée Taylor Swift motivated him to continue his NFL career, praising her creativity and passion. His comments quickly went viral, sparking mixed fan reactions online. While some admired the couple’s supportive dynamic, others responded with sarcasm and criticism, questioning his motives and joking about Swift influencing his decision to keep playing.

title: FBI is investigating malware-laden games on Steam: Names; timeline of attack and tips
description: The FBI is investigating malware embedded in games on Steam, with a focus on potential victims between May 2024 and January 2026. Several games, including BlockBlasters and Chemia, have been identified as containing the malicious software. The agency is urging anyone who may have installed these games or has relevant information to come forward.

title: $3.4 trillion mistake with China: Why US will make India's rise far tougher
description: The US has spent a staggering $3.4 trillion on a "militarised rivalry" with China since 2012, a figure dwarfing its Afghanistan war costs. This massive peacetime spending, driven by a strategic pivot to Asia, highlights Washington's determination to avoid repeating past "mistakes" with Beijing, particularly concerning technological transfers and economic engagement, as it now recalibrates its approach with India.
